name: Divider
description: A divider is a thin line that separates and groups content — between list items, sections of a form, or regions of a dense layout. Used sparingly, it brings order without adding weight.
keyRule: Reach for whitespace and grouping first; add a divider only where a clear boundary helps. Too many lines make a layout feel busy.

variants.name: Full-width
variants.whenToUse: Separate major sections edge to edge.

variants.name: Inset
variants.whenToUse: Between list items, aligned past the leading element.

variants.name: Middle
variants.whenToUse: Indented on both sides for a lighter separation.

variants.name: Vertical
variants.whenToUse: Separate inline items in a toolbar.

anatomy.name: Inset
anatomy.description: optional left/right indentation to align with content (e.g. past an avatar).

behaviors.name: Grouping
behaviors.description: Use a divider to mark a real boundary between groups, not between every item — rows already separate visually.

behaviors.name: Inset alignment
behaviors.description: Inset dividers align with the text column so the eye reads a clean left edge.

specs.value: 16px (or aligned to content, e.g. 72px past an avatar)

props.name: variant
props.description: Which divider treatment to render; derived from the spec's Variants table (full-width, inset, middle, vertical).

props.name: className
props.description: Optional extra class names to compose with the base divider class.

doThis: Use dividers to mark real group boundaries.
Inset to align with the content column.
Prefer spacing where it reads clearly without a line.
dontDoThis: Don't put a divider between every list row.
Don't use heavy or coloured rules for plain separation.
Don't rely on a divider to convey meaning by itself.
accessibility: Decorative dividers are hidden from assistive tech.
A divider that genuinely separates groups can use a separator role.
Never use a line as the only way to convey structure — pair with headings/labels.

related.name: List
related.why: Use the inset variant between list items

related.name: ExpansionPanel
related.why: Prefer structured grouping over long divider runs
